phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight edge at level 1: your reflect-barrier poke beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — reflect-barrier poke edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Slight edge at level 3: your reflect-barrier poke beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean your way — reflect-barrier poke edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight deficit at the level-6 breakpoint: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your reflect-barrier poke on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your reflect-barrier poke in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Slight deficit at two-plus items: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your reflect-barrier poke on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-volley his last-hits — the radiant barrage chips from a range most mids can't answer at one.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: Rebuttal turns his poke into YOUR poke. The reflect re-prices every projectile in the lane.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E completes the kit: the snare into full volley is your kill pattern. Hold it for real windows.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your reflect-barrier poke out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean your way — reflect-barrier poke edge out the stardust ramp control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Luden's spike: snare-volley chunks half bars and the execute cleans the remainder. Force contacts.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Mel is a counter-artillery battery: reflect their poke, snare their dive, and execute the chipped.

phases.why: Luden's spike: snare-volley chunks half bars and the execute cleans the remainder. Force contacts.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Mel is a counter-artillery battery: reflect their poke, snare their dive, and execute the chipped.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-volley his last-hits — the radiant barrage chips from a range most mids can't answer at one.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: Rebuttal turns his poke into YOUR poke. The reflect re-prices every projectile in the lane.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E completes the kit: the snare into full volley is your kill pattern. Hold it for real windows.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Lost Chapter timing — volley every wave contact and bank the overwhelm stacks on his health bar.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Golden Eclipse online — the execute threshold turns your chip war into a countdown he can see.
